mnemonics: F.E.W. Functionality: Focuses on Efficiency (Energy), Waste (E-Waste)
keyPoints: Functionality in Green IT refers to a system's ability to perform its intended tasks while meeting sustainability goals.
It balances performance requirements with energy efficiency and resource conservation.
Key aspects include optimizing software algorithms, enabling power-saving modes, and designing for longevity and repairability.
commonMistakes: Equating high performance with high energy consumption by default; efficient design can achieve both.
Overlooking software's role in hardware energy use; inefficient code can waste significant power.
Focusing only on initial manufacturing impact and ignoring the operational energy use throughout the product's lifecycle.
